texto: So is this being in love? I stay with the moment, waiting to find out, the space between us fluctuating with uncertainty. The only thing I am sure of is that each time his lips leave mine they are right back again.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Kea Alwang que provoca vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. Ao questionar “So is this being in love? I stay with the moment, waiting to find out, the space between us fluct…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amor, fantasy, kea-alwang, com foco em kea-alwang e romance.

texto: So I’m figuring this is death. The little air left in the cockpit is toxic with marthenine, and I can only wonder how much of it I have breathed in. Is my throat becoming raw hamburger? My lungs, oatmeal?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Kea Alwang que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “So I’m figuring this is death. The little air left in the cockpit is toxic with marthenine, and I…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, fantasy, kea-alwang, com foco em luto e kea-alwang.

frase_original: So I’m figuring this is death. The little air left in the cockpit is toxic with marthenine, and I can only wonder how much of it I have breathed in. Is my throat becoming raw hamburger? My lungs, oatmeal?

texto: That’s when I notice Cheryl and Mickey cuddled up on the couch. She’s leaning on his shoulder, his arm around her, her leg across his lap. Cheryl throws glances at Kerry that say, “Look at me!” while Kerry shoots a “You go, girl!” smirk right back. I think of CK, how he and I often sat like that. Not because we were seconds from making out or wanted to look like a couple, but just out of a deep, platonic connection. My heart hits a higher notch on the ache-o-meter, my teeth sear into my bottom lip, and then something inside me snaps as cleanly as a crayon.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Kea Alwang: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “That’s when I notice Cheryl and Mickey cuddled up on the couch. She’s leaning on his shoulder, hi…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, fantasy, kea-alwang pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de kea-alwang e science-fiction.

Cheryl throws glances at Kerry that say, “Look at me!” while Kerry shoots a “You go, girl!” smirk right back. I think of CK, how he and I often sat like that. Not because we were seconds from making out or wanted to look like a couple, but just out of a deep, platonic connection.
